title: Hypericum beanii

This classification places it as part of the order Malpighiales, which includes a diverse range of flowering plants. As a member of the Hypericum genus, it shares characteristics with other species, such as its ability to produce showy flowers and its preference for well-drained soils. intro: Hypericum beanii, also known as Bean's St. John's Wort, is a species of flowering plant in the St. John's Wort family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may contribute to its use in treating wounds, skin conditions, and anxiety-related disorders. The bioactive constituents of Hypericum beanii include flavonoids, such as rutin and hyperoside, as well as phenolic acids, which are thought to be responsible for its pharmacological activities. In herbal medicine, Bean's St. John's Wort is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical creams and salves to aid in wound healing and skin irritation.
